summary: Marijuana Legalization Initiative Legalize the possession, distribution, and use of marijuana for person aged 21 years old and above.
notes: Outcome: Failed. No vote percentages available.

summary: Right-to-Work Amendment Make it illegal for employers to mandate labor union membership for employees as a condition for employment.
notes: Outcome: Passed. No vote percentages available.

summary: Acting Governor Amendment Provide a process and line of succession for an acting Governor when and if the governor can no longer perform the duties and powers of their office.
notes: Outcome: Passed. No vote percentages available.

summary: Remove Slavery as Punishment for Crime from Constitution Amendment Remove language from the Tennessee Constitution that allowed the use of slavery and involuntary servitude as criminal punishments, and replace it with "Slavery and involuntary servitude are forever prohibited.".
notes: Outcome: Passed. No vote percentages available.

summary: Remove Religious Minister Disqualification Amendment Remove a section of the Tennessee Constitution that disqualified religious ministers from being elected to the Tennessee General Assembly
notes: Outcome: Passed. No vote percentages available.

summary: Property Tax Limit Reduction for Elderly and Disabled Residents Amendment Allow the Legislature to lower property tax limits for school maintenance and operations taxes paid by elderly or disabled people.
notes: Outcome: Passed. No vote percentages available.

summary: Increased Homestead Exemption for School District Property Taxes Amendment Increase homestead exemption value for school district property taxes from $25,000 to $40,000.
notes: Outcome: Passed. No vote percentages available.

summary: Emergency Session Appropriation Limits Measure Increases the approprations limit of an emergency session the Legislature can hold from 1 percent to 5 percent of the previous fiscal year's budget. Exempt emergency federal funding from this limit. Exempt any appropriations that lowered total spending for the year from this limit.
notes: Outcome: Failed. No vote percentages available.

summary: Prohibit Slavery and Indentured Servitude Amendment Removes language in the State Constitution that allowed slavery or indentured servitude. Ban slavery and indentured servitude in the State Constitution.
notes: Outcome: Passed. No vote percentages available.

summary: Right to Personal Reproductive Autonomy Amendment Protects the right to personal reproductive autonomy Prohibit government infringement on personal reproductive autonomy unless there is a "compelling State interest" .
notes: Outcome: Passed. No vote percentages available.

summary: Aircraft Fuel Tax Increase Nonbinding Question Maintain a tax increase on aircraft from 11 cents per gallon to 18 cents per gallon, generating an estimated $14 million in the first ten years, from Senate Bill 5974 of 2022
notes: Outcome: Repealed. No vote percentages available.

summary: Increase Minimum Wage for Tipped Employees Measure Phase out the minimum wage exemption for tipped employees . Match minimum hourly wage of tipped workers to that of non-tipped workers by July 1, 2027.
notes: Outcome: Passed. No vote percentages available.

summary: No Court Authority over Impeachment Amendment Prohibit state courts from having authority over impeachment proceedings by the West Virginia Legislature . Prohibit state courts from reviewing impeachment judgments made by the West Virginia Senate .
notes: Outcome: Failed. No vote percentages available.

summary: Authorize Tax Exemptions for Vehicles and Personal Property Used for Business Amendment Authorize the West Virginia Legislature to exempt personal property used for business activity from property taxes .
notes: Outcome: Failed. No vote percentages available.

summary: Incorporation of Religious Denominations and Churches Amendment Allow the West Virginia Legislature to incorporate new churches and religious denominations.
notes: Outcome: Failed. No vote percentages available.

summary: Legislative Approval of the State Board of Education Rules Amendment Require the West Virginia Board of Education to submit its rules and policies to the West Virginia Legislature for approval, amendment, or rejection.
notes: Outcome: Failed. No vote percentages available.

summary: Local Government Stock Investing Amendment Allow the Wyoming Legislature to provide that local governments can invest in stock and equities . This would require a two-thirds vote from both houses of the Legislature.
notes: Outcome: Passed. No vote percentages available.
